caseSummary: 35-year-old recreational runner presenting with proximal hamstring tendinopathy. Onset approximately 6 weeks ago, likely related to a recent increase in running volume (added weekly long run). Pain is localised to the right sit bone area, rated 7/10 at worst during loading activities and currently 4/10. Aggravated by running, sitting for extended periods, and hip flexion under load. Able to walk without significant pain. No neurological symptoms reported.
onset: 6 weeks ago, gradual onset coinciding with increased training load
currentActivityTolerance: Can walk comfortably. Short, flat runs below 20 minutes tolerable at low effort. Unable to run at normal training pace or distance without pain flare.
keyConstraints: Avoid positions of hip flexion under load (e.g. deadlifts, seated stretching)
Limit sitting on hard surfaces without padding
No aggressive hamstring stretching — may worsen tendinopathy
missingInformation: Whether imaging (ultrasound or MRI) has been performed to confirm diagnosis
Previous episode history — first occurrence or recurrent?
Whether physiotherapy has been consulted previously
uncertaintyNotes: Diagnosis is user-reported; clinical confirmation by physiotherapist recommended
Exact running volume increase not quantified
